originalTitle: Ex-Nigeria oil minister cleared in UK bribery trial
title: Big News About Fairness from Nigeria!
content:
Hello Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you some important news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have a very special story. It's about a kind lady from a country called Nigeria. It's a big story about rules, fairness, and telling the truth. Let's learn about it together!

A Lady from Nigeria

Our story is about a kind lady named Diezani Alison-Madueke. That's a big name, isn't it? She used to have a very important job in Nigeria. She was like a super helper for her country! Her job was to look after something very special and important for Nigeria: its oil. Imagine having a job where you help manage a big treasure for your whole country! That's what she did.

What is Oil?

Now, you might ask, 'What is oil?' Well, oil is a very special liquid that comes from deep, deep under the ground. It's black and gooey when it first comes out, but it's super valuable! People call it 'black gold' sometimes because it's so important. Did you know that oil helps many things work? It helps cars and buses drive to school. It helps big airplanes fly in the sky. It can even help make electricity so we can have lights in our homes and watch TV! Nigeria has a lot of this special oil, and it helps the country earn money to build schools, hospitals, and roads. So, looking after the oil is a very, very important job!

A Big Problem Came Up

One day, a big problem came up. Some people said that our friend, Diezani, did something wrong. They said she took secret money from other people. These other people were very rich because they also worked with oil. We call them 'oil tycoons' – big bosses in the oil business. Taking secret money to do something unfair is called 'bribery.' It's like when someone gives you a sweet to make you let them go first in line, even when it's not their turn. That's not fair, is it? So, people said Diezani took bribes. This made many people wonder what happened.

How Problems Are Solved in Court

When there's a big problem like this, we need special places to help figure it out. These places are called 'courts.' A court is like a big, serious classroom where people go to talk about rules and disagreements. In a court, everyone gets a chance to tell their side of the story. There are special people in the court, like the judge, who makes sure everyone follows the rules. It's very important to find out the truth and make sure everything is fair. This story happened in a court in a far-away country called the United Kingdom, or the UK for short. It's a country across the big ocean.

The Special Helpers: The Jury

In this special court, there was a group of people called a 'jury.' The jury is made up of ordinary people, just like your mommy, daddy, or your neighbours! They are not judges or lawyers. Their job is super important. Did you know the jury's job is to listen very carefully to *all* the stories? They listen to what the people saying 'she did something wrong' say, and they listen to what Diezani says. They look at all the papers and clues. After listening to everything, the jury talks together and decides if someone is 'guilty' or 'not guilty.' It's like they are super detectives trying to find the truth!

The Happy News! Not Guilty!

So, the jury listened and listened for a very long time. They thought very hard about everything. And guess what? They made a very important decision! The jury said that Diezani Alison-Madueke was 'not guilty.' This means the jury decided that she did *not* take the secret money. They decided she did *not* do anything wrong. This is very big news! It means that the accusations, the ideas that she did something bad, were not true after all. She is innocent! It's like when your friend said you took their crayon, but then they found it in their own bag, and you didn't take it after all. You were innocent!

What This Means

When someone is found 'not guilty,' it means they are free. They are not in trouble for that problem anymore. The court has looked at everything, and the special jury has made their decision. It's a big relief for everyone involved. This shows us how important it is to have courts and juries. They help make sure that everyone gets a fair chance. It reminds us that we should not jump to conclusions. We should wait for all the facts before deciding if someone did something wrong. It is a big lesson about justice and fairness for everyone.

Why This Matters for Nigeria

This story is important for Nigeria and for all of us in Africa. It shows that even when big problems come up, and important people are involved, the rules of fairness still apply. It shows that courts work hard to find the truth. Did you know that honesty and fairness are very important for any country to be strong and happy? When everyone follows the rules and tries to be fair, then the country can grow and do amazing things. This story reminds us that telling the truth and making fair decisions is super important for everyone, everywhere.

A Message for Super Kids!

So, my super friends, what can we learn from this big story? We learn that it's always important to be honest and fair in everything you do. Always tell the truth, even if it's hard. And remember, when someone says something bad about someone else, it's good to listen to all sides and wait for the truth to come out, just like the jury did. Let's all promise to be honest and fair every day. You are the future of Africa, and by being fair and kind, you make our continent an even better place to live! Keep shining bright, little stars!
summary: A former important lady from Nigeria, Diezani Alison-Madueke, who used to manage the country's oil, was accused of taking secret money, but after a long trial in a court, a special group of people called a jury found her not guilty, meaning she did nothing wrong.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how courts and juries work to be fair and find the truth, reminding us that being honest and following rules is very important for everyone in a country.

originalTitle: Al Jazeera journalist reunited with family in Khartoum after years of war
title: A Big Happy Hug for Al-Tahir's Family!
content:
A Happy Welcome Home!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! We have some super happy news to share with you today. Imagine waiting a very, very long time to hug someone you love so much. Well, for a special dad named Al-Tahir, that wait is finally over! He is home and back with his family. Everyone is smiling and so, so happy! It is a big, happy day in a city called Khartoum. This is a story about love, family, and how good it feels when people can be together again. It reminds us of the power of hugs and how important it is to have your loved ones close to you. Let's learn more about this special reunion!

Who is Al-Tahir? Our Brave Storyteller!

Al-Tahir is a very brave man. He has a very important job. He is a "journalist." Do you know what a journalist does? A journalist is like a special storyteller for the whole wide world! They go to different places to find out what is happening. They ask people questions and listen carefully. Then, they share these stories with everyone. They write words, or they show pictures and videos on TV. Al-Tahir works for a big news channel called Al Jazeera. This channel tells stories from all over the world, even right here in Africa! Journalists help us learn new things. They help us understand what is going on in our big, amazing world. They help us stay informed and sometimes, they even help us feel hopeful. **Did you know?** Journalists are like friendly detectives! They look for important information and share it with everyone so we can all know what's happening.

Why Was Al-Tahir Away? A Long, Sad Time

Al-Tahir had to be away from his family for a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? Three whole years! That's like going from when you started Class 2 all the way until you might be in Class 5! Imagine not seeing your mom, dad, brothers, or sisters for so long. It must have been very hard and very sad for everyone. Why was he away? Because of something called "war." War is when people fight each other in a big way. It makes places not safe for anyone. When there is war, things can get broken, and homes can be in danger. It makes people scared, and sometimes families have to go to different places to stay safe. Sometimes, brave people like Al-Tahir have to keep working even when it's not safe, to help tell the stories of what is happening. War is a very sad thing because it stops people from living happily together. It breaks things and makes many people cry. Everyone wishes for peace instead of war. **Did you know?** Peace means everyone can live happily and safely together, without fighting or being scared. It's what everyone wishes for!

Where is Khartoum? A City in Africa!

Al-Tahir's family lives in a city called Khartoum. Khartoum is a very important city in a country called Sudan. Sudan is a big country right here in Africa, just like our own! Khartoum is like the busy heart of Sudan. It's a place with many tall buildings, lively markets, and lots of schools. Many people live and work there every day. But even in a big city like Khartoum, war can cause big problems and make life very difficult. Al-Tahir's family was there, waiting and waiting, hoping and hoping that he would come back home soon. They missed him every single day for those three long years. **Did you know?** Khartoum is a special city because two big rivers, called the Blue Nile and the White Nile, meet there! It's a meeting point for nature too!

The Happy Reunion! Big Hugs and Smiles!

After all that long, sad time, things got a little bit calmer. And guess what? Al-Tahir finally got to go home! Imagine the moment when he walked through the door! Maybe his children ran to him with the biggest smiles and open arms, ready for a huge hug! Imagine lots and lots of hugs! His wife must have smiled and laughed with pure joy. Being "reunited" means coming back together after being apart for a long time. It's like finding a super special toy you lost, but even, even better! Because it's a person you love so much! When Al-Tahir saw his family, his heart must have felt so full of happiness and love. Now, they can talk and play, eat meals together, and tell each other all the stories from the past three years. It’s a time for big celebrations, lots of love, and making up for all the lost time. The family can now make many, many new happy memories together!

Why is This Story Important?

This story reminds us all how super important families are. Families are like a strong team. They help each other. They laugh together. They love each other more than anything! No matter what happens, families want to be together and support one another. This story also shows us that even after very sad times, happy times can come back. It shows us that peace is a wonderful, wonderful thing. When there is peace, families can stay together. Children can go to school safely every day. People can work without being scared. Everyone wants peace! Peace helps our communities grow strong, safe, and happy. Al-Tahir’s reunion is a wonderful sign of hope. It tells us that even after tough times, good things can happen. It tells us to keep wishing for peace for everyone, everywhere. Every family in Africa and all over the world deserves to be safe, together, and full of love. **Did you know?** In many African cultures, "family" is very big! It includes not just your mom and dad and siblings, but also grandparents, aunts, uncles, and all your cousins! It's a huge, loving community!

Our Wish for Everyone!

So, let's all smile big for Al-Tahir and his family in Khartoum! They are together again, and that is truly wonderful news. Let’s keep hoping and wishing for more and more families in Africa and all over the world to be safe, together, and happy. Because a happy family means a happy community, and happy communities make a happy world!
summary: Journalist Al-Tahir al-Mardi is finally back with his family in Khartoum after three long years apart because of war, bringing big smiles and hugs.
whyMatters: This story shows how important families are and why we should always hope for peace so everyone can be safe and together.

originalTitle: Why was Kane's penalty retaken against Croatia?
title: Big Football News! Harry Kane Gets a Second Chance!
content:
Hey there, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly reporter here, ready to tell you a super exciting story from the wonderful world of football! You know football, right? The game where players kick a ball and try to score goals! It's played all over the world, and we love it so much here in our amazing continent! Today, we have a fun story about a very famous football player named Harry Kane. He plays for a team called England. And something very interesting happened in one of his big games! Get ready to learn about fair play, special rules, and how cool technology helps make the game even better!

What is Football?

First, let's remember what football is all about! It's a game played with two teams. Each team tries to kick a ball into the other team's goal. The team with the most goals wins! It's a super fun game where players run, kick, and work together as a team. Maybe you play football with your friends too! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! Billions of people love watching and playing it!

Meet Harry Kane!

Our story today is about a very talented player called Harry Kane. He is a captain for the England team. He is very good at scoring goals! Imagine someone who is really good at what they do, like you might be good at drawing or telling stories. Harry Kane is like that, but with football!

What is a Penalty Kick?

Sometimes, during a football game, a player on one team might do something wrong inside a special box near the goal. This is called a foul. When a big foul happens in this special box, the other team gets a very special kick! It's called a penalty kick. A penalty kick is taken from a special spot, usually 11 big steps away from the goal. Only one player from the kicking team gets to try and score, and only the goalkeeper from the other team tries to stop it. It's a really good chance to score a goal! Did you know? The penalty spot is 11 metres (or about 12 yards) from the goal line. That's like the length of two big cars!

A Big Game: England vs. Croatia

Harry Kane was playing a very important game with his England team. They were playing against another strong team called Croatia. Both teams wanted to win very much! During this exciting game, something happened inside Croatia's special goal box. A foul was committed! So, the referee, who is like the judge of the game, blew his whistle! He pointed to the penalty spot. This meant England got a penalty kick!

Harry Kane's First Kick!

Harry Kane, being the great goal scorer he is, stepped up to take the penalty kick. He got ready to kick the ball with all his might! He kicked the ball! And guess what? He scored a goal! Everyone on the England team was super happy! They thought they had the lead! But then, something a little unusual happened. The referee held up his hand. He looked like he was listening to someone through an earpiece! Hmm, what could it be?

What is VAR?

This is where a super cool new helper comes in! It's called VAR. It stands for Video Assistant Referee. Imagine you are watching a football game on TV. You can see replays, right? You can watch things again in slow motion! VAR is like having extra eyes for the referee. There are special cameras all around the football field. And there are other referees watching these cameras in a special room. If something big and important happens that the referee on the field might have missed, the VAR team can tell him! They watch all the replays carefully. Did you know? VAR was first used in big football games only a few years ago! It helps make sure the game is fair for everyone, just like making sure everyone takes turns when playing with friends!

How VAR Helps the Referee

So, in Harry Kane's game, the VAR team in their special room saw something important during the penalty kick. They saw it on their many screens! They told the referee on the field through his earpiece, "Hold on! Something might be wrong!" The referee then had a choice. He could listen to VAR, or he could even go to a special TV screen on the side of the field to watch the replay himself! He went to the screen! He watched the replay very, very carefully. He looked at what happened just before Harry Kane kicked the ball.

The Big Rule: No Early Entering!

Here's the tricky part! When a penalty kick is taken, all the players (except the kicker and the goalkeeper) must stay outside the special penalty box. They must wait until the ball is kicked! If a player from *either* team runs into the box *before* the ball is kicked, then it's against the rules! Especially if that player then gets involved in the game right after. The VAR team saw that some players from *both* England and Croatia ran into the box a little bit too early! Just before Harry Kane kicked the ball!

Harry Kane Gets a Second Chance!

Because players from both teams ran in too early, and because Harry Kane scored, the rule says the penalty kick must be taken *again*! Can you imagine? Harry Kane had scored, everyone was celebrating, but now he had to do it all over again! That takes a lot of bravery and focus! The referee blew his whistle again, pointing to the spot. Harry Kane took a deep breath. He knew he had to focus one more time. He ran up and kicked the ball again! This time, he scored again! Hooray! And this time, no one ran into the box too early. So, England got their goal, thanks to Harry Kane's two kicks and VAR making sure the rules were followed perfectly! It was exciting to see him get a second chance and score! Did you know? It takes a lot of courage to take a penalty kick, especially when everyone is watching and the pressure is high! Harry Kane showed great courage!

Why This Matters: Fair Play!

This story shows us something very important about football and about life: fair play! VAR helps make sure that the rules are followed fairly for both teams. It means that the game is decided by skill, not by mistakes or things that aren't quite right. It also shows us that even big, strong players like Harry Kane have to follow the rules, and sometimes they get a second chance if things aren't perfect the first time. Technology like VAR is helping to make our favourite games even more fair and exciting! Did you know? In many African countries, children love football so much that they sometimes make their own balls from plastic bags, old clothes, or even socks! They still play with lots of skill and joy!

Keep Loving Football!

So, next time you watch a football game, or kick a ball with your friends, remember Harry Kane's story! Remember that fair play is important, and sometimes, even with a little help from technology, everyone gets a chance to play by the rules. Keep cheering for your favourite teams, and keep playing the beautiful game! Maybe one day you'll be a famous football player too, representing your amazing country! Keep shining, super kids!
summary: In a big football game, famous player Harry Kane scored a penalty kick, but it had to be taken again because a special TV helper called VAR saw that some players ran into the box too early, making sure the rules were followed fairly.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us about fair play in football and how new technology like VAR helps referees make sure games are played by the rules, giving everyone a fair chance to win.
